summary_html:
New Features

• Scripted Calls with Bare Flag
Claude Code 2.1.81 adds a --bare flag for scripted -p calls that skips hooks, LSP, and plugin sync, plus a --channels permission relay for forwarding tool approvals to your phone.
Read on Anthropic Docs
• Rate Limits in Statusline Display
A new rate_limits field for statusline scripts lets you display Claude.ai rate limit usage directly in your terminal statusline, alongside a new source: 'settings' option for declaring plugin entries inline in settings.json.
Read on Anthropic Docs
• Console Auth and Remote Control
Added a --console flag to claude auth login for Anthropic Console authentication, a Show turn duration toggle in /config, and a /remote-control command in VSCode to bridge sessions to claude.ai/code.
Read on Anthropic Docs
• StopFailure Hooks and Plugin State
New StopFailure hook event fires on turn-end API errors, and a ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_DATA} variable enables persistent state for plugins, with effort, maxTurns, and disallowedTools frontmatter support added for plugin agents.
Read on Anthropic Docs
• Opus 4.6 Default Token Boost
Claude Code 2.1.77 raises the default maximum output token limit for Opus 4.6 to 64k tokens, with an upper bound of 128k, and adds allowRead sandbox filesystem settings and an optional index for the /copy command.
Read on Anthropic Docs

General News

• Mobile Cowork Control via Thread
Pro and Max plan users can now access a persistent agent thread via Claude Desktop or Claude for iOS/Android to manage and assign Cowork tasks remotely from anywhere.
Read on Anthropic
• Interactive Charts in Responses
Claude can now create custom charts, diagrams, and other visualizations inline in its responses, bringing richer visual output directly into chat.
Read on Anthropic
• Excel and PowerPoint Context Sharing
The Claude for Excel and Claude for PowerPoint add-ins now share full conversation context across both apps, so actions in one are informed by everything that happened in the other, with added support for skills and LLM gateway connections.
Read on Anthropic
• Memory Now Free for All Users
Memory from chat history is now available to all Claude users including those on the free plan, allowing Claude to remember details across conversations.
Read on Anthropic
• Scheduled Tasks in Cowork
Cowork now supports creating and scheduling both recurring and on-demand tasks, and a new Customize section in Claude Desktop groups skills, plugins, and connectors in one place.
Read on Anthropic

New Versions

The latest version of Claude Code is: v2.1.81 GitHub Releases

tip.description: Check your current token usage and see how much context window remains. Useful for long sessions.
